{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27212","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27212/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27212/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27212/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27212","id":2465378354,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86S8rAy","number":27212,"title":"ENH: do not iterate over dict-like during array creation (inconsistent with dict)","user":{"login":"rgbmrc","id":33603991,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzNjAzOTkx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33603991?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc","html_url":"https://github.com/rgbmrc","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rgbmrc/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359239,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjM5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/01%20-%20Enhancement","name":"01 - Enhancement","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-14T09:45:54Z","updated_at":"2024-09-08T14:08:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Proposed new feature or change:

Calling `np.asarray` (or similar) on a (lists of) `dict` objects creates an object array. Same for instances of classes derived from `dict`. Conversely, for objects inheriting from `collections.abc.MutableMapping` (including `collections.UserDict`) it iterates through the dictionary keys. I think it makes sense not to iterate over any `MutableMapping`.

#### Current behviour:
tested with numpy: 2.0.1, python: 3.12.5 | packaged by conda-forge | (main, Aug 8 2024, 18:36:51) [GCC 12.4.0]
```[python]
import numpy as np
from collections import UserDict

class Dict(dict):
    pass

print(repr(np.asarray([dict({1: 1, 2: 2})])))  # array([{1: 1, 2: 2}], dtype=object)
print(repr(np.asarray([Dict({1: 1, 2: 2})])))  # array([{1: 1, 2: 2}], dtype=object)
print(repr(np.asarray([UserDict({1: 1, 2: 2})])))  # array([[1, 2]])
```
With the proposed change, UserDict behaves like the
